title: LLM-Actionable BDD Acceptance Criteria Specification
description: The complete and authoritative source of truth for platform behavior, designed to be used as a direct input prompt for an LLM to generate or update corresponding code.
specificationGuidelines: Complete and authoritative source of truth.
No undocumented behavior, architecture, UX, or technical assumptions.
Only explicitly specified behaviors and failure modes should be implemented.
Observable behavior must exactly satisfy the criteria (technology-agnostic).

specification_principles.title: Specification Principles
specification_principles.description: The operational context and principles for the implementation.
const: The codebase is a high-quality, BDD-verified implementation of the feature.
The implementation adheres strictly to this specification; no undocumented behavior or technical detail is present.
This JSON is the authoritative source of truth for the system's behavior and structure.
The initialization command provides a reproducible development environment.
The verification command ensures continuous compliance with all acceptance criteria.
Type safety, idiomatic patterns, and exhaustive test coverage are fundamental characteristics of the codebase.
The observable behavior is identical to the behavior_acceptance_criteria.

feature.title: Feature Name
feature.description: High-level name of the feature or behavior being specified.

software_requirements.title: Software Requirements
software_requirements.description: A list of software required to run and verify the codebase.

initialization_command.title: Initialization Command
initialization_command.description: The command that initializes the repository from a template.

verification_command.title: Verification Command
verification_command.description: The command that continuously runs DEBUG=1 nix run and verifies implementation progress. The DEBUG=1 nix run command should not output any warning/error from any of the commands.

extra_allowed_commands.title: Extra Allowed Commands
extra_allowed_commands.description: A list of additional commands allowed for development and maintenance.

implementation_acceptance_criteria.title: Implementation Acceptance Criteria
implementation_acceptance_criteria.description: Technical requirements that the implementation must satisfy, including test coverage.

behavior_acceptance_criteria.title: Behavior Acceptance Criteria
behavior_acceptance_criteria.description: A list of authoritative scenarios that define the complete observable behavior.

specification_principles: The codebase is a high-quality, BDD-verified implementation of the feature.
The implementation adheres strictly to this specification; no undocumented behavior or technical detail is present.
This JSON is the authoritative source of truth for the system's behavior and structure.
The initialization command provides a reproducible development environment.
The verification command ensures continuous compliance with all acceptance criteria.
Type safety, idiomatic patterns, and exhaustive test coverage are fundamental characteristics of the codebase.
The observable behavior is identical to the behavior_acceptance_criteria.
implementation_acceptance_criteria: The application should use Rust's built-in testing framework for unit testing.
The application should use Nix for environment reproducibility.
The application should use Rust for type safety.
The DEBUG=1 nix run command should not output any warning/error from any of the commands.

when.statement: I run the application without arguments or environment variables

then.statement: the application output should be 'Hello, world!'

when.statement: I run the application with the environment variable 'DEBUG=1'

then.statement: the application output should include 'test hello_world ... ok'
the application output should include 'test math ... ok'

behavior_scenario.title: Behavioral Scenario
behavior_scenario.description: A single observable behavior defined by conditions, triggers, and outcomes.

name.title: Scenario Name
name.description: Descriptive name of the success or failure scenario.

given.title: Givens
given.description: Initial conditions and preconditions required for the scenario.

when.title: Thens
when.description: Events and actions that trigger the scenario.

then.title: Thens
then.description: Expected outcomes and observable changes in system state.

clause.title: Behavioral Clause
clause.description: A single atomic statement forming part of a BDD scenario.

type.title: Clause Type
type.description: The logic role of the clause in the BDD structure.

statement.title: Clause Statement
statement.description: The descriptive content of the behavior or condition.
